edges.description: Partial enzyme deficiency drives combined visceral and partial neuronal storage.
edges.source: Partial Acid Sphingomyelinase Deficiency from SMPD1 Variants

edges.description: Reticuloendothelial sphingomyelin storage and foam-cell infiltration enlarge the spleen as part of chronic neurovisceral ASMD.
Hepatic reticuloendothelial sphingomyelin storage and foam-cell infiltration enlarge the liver as part of chronic neurovisceral ASMD.
Intermediate residual enzyme activity can leave nervous-system involvement alongside non-neurologic storage disease.
Pulmonary sphingomyelin storage and inflammatory-cell infiltration contribute to chronic interstitial lung disease in ASMD.

edges.target: Partial Acid Sphingomyelinase Deficiency from SMPD1 Variants

nodes.description: Hepatomegaly accompanies splenomegaly as part of the visceral storage burden.

evidence.reference_title: Types A and B Niemann-Pick disease.
evidence.snippet: Type B patients also have hepatosplenomegaly and pathologic alterations of their lungs

nodes.description: Sphingomyelin accumulates in reticuloendothelial macrophages (spleen, liver, lung, marrow) and, because residual enzyme is insufficient to fully protect neurons, also variably in the nervous system, producing the combined neurovisceral picture of intermediate ASMD.

evidence.reference_title: Types A and B Niemann-Pick disease.
evidence.snippet: varying degrees of lipid storage and foam cell infiltration in tissues

nodes.description: Progressive interstitial lung disease from pulmonary storage macrophages.

evidence.reference_title: Disease manifestations and burden of illness in patients with acid sphingomyelinase deficiency (ASMD).
evidence.snippet: most patients have interstitial lung disease with progressive impairment of pulmonary function

nodes.description: Later-onset and variable neurological involvement (e.g., developmental delay, ataxia, or slowly progressive deterioration) distinguishes the intermediate form from non-neuronopathic type B.

evidence.reference_title: Types A and B Niemann-Pick disease.
evidence.snippet: Intermediate patients also have been reported with mild to moderate neurological findings.

nodes.description: Biallelic SMPD1 variants leave intermediate residual acid sphingomyelinase activity, greater than in infantile type A but insufficient for normal sphingomyelin turnover. The residual activity partially protects the CNS, giving an intermediate neurovisceral phenotype rather than the fatal infantile course.
nodes.id: Partial Acid Sphingomyelinase Deficiency from SMPD1 Variants

evidence.reference_title: Types A and B Niemann-Pick disease.
evidence.snippet: All patients with types A and B NPD have mutations in the gene encoding ASM (SMPD1), and thus the disease is more accurately referred to as ASM deficiency (ASMD).

meta.association: Biallelic SMPD1 variants with intermediate residual acid sphingomyelinase activity

evidence.reference_title: Disease manifestations and burden of illness in patients with acid sphingomyelinase deficiency (ASMD).
evidence.snippet: Acid sphingomyelinase deficiency (ASMD), a rare lysosomal storage disease, is an autosomal recessive genetic disorder caused by different SMPD1 mutations.

nodes.description: Splenomegaly is a near-constant feature of chronic ASMD.

evidence.reference_title: Types A and B Niemann-Pick disease.
evidence.snippet: Type B patients also have hepatosplenomegaly and pathologic alterations of their lungs
